title: jatropha diversifolia var. pandurifolia
intro: Jatropha diversifolia var. pandurifolia, commonly known as the Paliwalo or Kaya-kaya, is a plant species found in the tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific. It is a shrub or small tree with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The plant is valued for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antihistamine properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of allergies, skin irritations, and infections. Herbal preparations of Jatropha diversifolia var. pandurifolia include infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, cough, and skin conditions, such as eczema and acne.
